zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard James Hurlbut於1548年出生在英國維爾爾布拉頓時，他的父親William Hulbert58歲，母親Elizabeth Burley25歲。他於1573年與Elizabeth Oxford在英國維爾爾布拉頓結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1619年在他的家鄉享年71歲，埋葬在英國薩默塞特布盧頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard James Hurlbut于1548年出生在英国維爾爾布拉頓时，他的父亲William Hulbert58岁，母亲Elizabeth Burley25岁。他于1573年与Elizabeth Oxford在英国維爾爾布拉頓结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1619年在他的家乡享年71岁，埋葬在英国薩默塞特布盧頓。

nameMeanings.name: Hurlbut
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from a medieval game called hurlebatte, which took its name from ‘short battes of a cubit long and a halfe, with pykes of yron, and were tied to a line, that when they were throwne, one might plucke them again’ (1565–73). Compare ‘Pleying at The two hande swerd, at swerd and bokelere, and at two pyked staf, at The hurlebatte’ (about 1450 in MED).

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Richard James Hurlbut was born in 1548, in Bratton, Wiltshire, England, his father, William Hulbert, was 58 and his mother, Elizabeth Burley, was 25. He married Elizabeth Oxford in 1573, in Bratton, Wiltshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1619, in his hometown, at the age of 71, and was buried in Bruton, Somerset, England.

Il a épousé Elizabeth Oxford en 1573, à Bratton, Wiltshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1619, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 71 ans, et a été enterré à Bruton, Somerset, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard James Hurlbut(이)가 1548년 영국 윌츠히르 브래턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Hulbert(은)는 58세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Burley(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1573년 영국 윌츠히르 브래턴에서 Elizabeth Oxford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1619년 그의 고향에서 71세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 서머세트 브루턴에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard James Hurlbut родился в 1548 г. в местности браттон, уилцхир, Англия, его отцу по имени William Hulbert было 58 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Burley, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Oxford в 1573 г. в местности браттон, уилцхир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1619 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 71 год и был похоронен в местности брутон, сомирсет, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard James Hurlbut nació en 1548, en Bratton, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William Hulbert, tenía 58 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Burley, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Oxford en 1573, en Bratton, Wiltshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1619, en su pueblo, a la edad de 71 años, y fue sepultado en Bruton, Somerset, Inglaterra.
